about.barangaysIntro: Koronadal is made up of 27 barangays, the largest of which include Gen. Paulino Santos, Santa Cruz, Sto. Niño, Zone III, and Zulueta.
about.glanceCapitalDesc: Seat of government of the Province of South Cotabato.
about.glanceCityhoodDesc: Converted into a component city under RA 8803.
about.glanceRegionalDesc: Center and seat of Region XII (SOCCSKSARGEN).
about.glanceResidentsDesc: Population based on the 2024 PSA census.
about.historyLink: Read the full history of Koronadal
about.historyP1: Koronadal began as the Marbel Settlement District, opened in the late 1930s to resettle landless farmers on the wide cogon plains of the Koronadal Valley. The first eighteen pioneer settlers arrived in 1940 and, with their families, quickly grew into a thriving community.
about.historyP2: The Municipality of Koronadal was created through Executive Order No. 82 in 1947, with the district of Marbel as its seat. In 1966, Republic Act No. 4849 made Koronadal the capital of the newly created Province of South Cotabato.
about.historyP3: Koronadal was converted into a component city through Republic Act No. 8803, ratified by plebiscite in 2000, and was later designated the regional center of SOCCSKSARGEN (Region XII).
about.introCapital: is the capital of the Province of
about.introResidents: it is home to a fast-growing community at the heart of southern Mindanao.
about.linkContactDesc: City Hall address, hotlines, and email.
about.linkServicesDesc: Health, business, civil registry, and more.
about.seoDescription: Learn about the City of Koronadal (Marbel), capital of South Cotabato and regional center of SOCCSKSARGEN: population, land area, barangays, history, and key facts.
about.seoKeywords: Koronadal, Marbel, South Cotabato, SOCCSKSARGEN, Hub of the South, about Koronadal, Koronadal city profile, Koronadal population, Koronadal barangays
about.seoTitle: About Koronadal City — Profile, History & Quick Facts
about.source1Note: Official city profile, history, and barangay information.

history.cityEraRange: From the conversion to a city in 2000 to the present
history.intro: From the cogon plains of a pre-war settlement district to a thriving component city and regional center, Koronadal's story spans more than eight decades of pioneering, struggle, and growth.
history.linkAboutDesc: City profile, quick facts, and barangays.
history.linkTourismDesc: Places to see and things to do in Koronadal.
history.mayorsIntro: The mayors who led Koronadal from the Marbel Settlement District through municipal government and into the city era.
history.mayorsNote: List compiled from the official city history and public records; years are approximate and may overlap during succession or transition periods.
history.municipalEraRange: From the settlement district to cityhood
history.nameKoronadal: From the Blaan words "koron" (cogon grass) and "nadal" (wide plain) — a fitting description of the open grassy valley the pioneers first settled.
history.nameMarbel: The older name of the settlement, still widely used today, believed to come from the Blaan "malb-el," referring to the murky waters of the local river.
history.pioneersIntro: The first eighteen pioneers who arrived in 1940 and laid the foundations of the community that would become Koronadal.
history.seoDescription: Explore the history of Koronadal (Marbel): the Marbel Settlement District, the pioneer settlers, EO 82 (1947), capital of South Cotabato (RA 4849, 1966), and cityhood (RA 8803, 2000).
history.seoKeywords: Koronadal history, Marbel, Marbel Settlement District, EO 82, RA 4849, RA 8803, South Cotabato history, SOCCSKSARGEN, Koronadal mayors, pioneer settlers
history.seoTitle: History of Koronadal City — From Marbel Settlement to Cityhood
history.source1Note: Official account of the city's founding, milestones, and mayors.
history.source3Note: Created the Municipality of Koronadal with Marbel as its seat.
history.source4Note: Created South Cotabato and made Koronadal its capital.
history.source5Note: Converted Koronadal into a component city of South Cotabato.
history.timelineIntro: The defining moments that shaped Koronadal, from its founding as a settlement district to its rise as the regional center of SOCCSKSARGEN.

demographics.howToReadBody: Census figures are actual resident counts taken by the PSA, not estimates. The annual growth rate is the average yearly change between two census years. Per-barangay figures are from the 2020 census; barangay-level 2024 counts have not yet been published. Koronadal's {{population}} residents make up about {{share}}% of South Cotabato's population.
demographics.introBefore: How many people call Koronadal home, where they live, and how the city has grown — drawn from the official
demographics.seoDescription: Population statistics for the City of Koronadal: PSA census history from 2000 to 2024, annual growth rates, and a breakdown of all 27 barangays.
demographics.seoKeywords: Koronadal population, Koronadal demographics, PSA census, Koronadal barangays population, South Cotabato statistics
demographics.title: Demographics

income.chartNote: Figures through 2016 are annual regular income; the {{year}} figure is total revenue per the BLGF Statement of Receipts and Expenditures. The basis differs slightly, so read the trend as indicative rather than a like-for-like series.
income.compositionBody: A line-item breakdown — locally sourced revenue (business tax, real property tax, non-tax revenue) versus the National Tax Allotment — is not yet published in a machine-readable form for FY {{year}}. The full itemization is available in the city's annual appropriation ordinance and the BLGF SRE; this page will be expanded once those figures are confirmed.
income.introBefore: Where the city's money comes from, drawn from the
income.seoDescription: The City of Koronadal's finances: total revenue, income classification, and the long-run trend in annual income, drawn from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF).
income.seoKeywords: Koronadal city income, Koronadal budget, BLGF revenue, local government finance, NTA Koronadal
income.title: City Income

tourism.aboutCardBody: Learn the city's history, geography, and the story behind its name.
tourism.cultureCardBody: Read about Koronadal's festivals, heritage, and cultural values on the official city website.

lakeHolon.description: A pristine crater lake on Mt. Melibingoy reached by a guided trek — turquoise waters, camping, and stargazing for more adventurous visitors.
lakeHolon.name: Lake Holon (Lake Maughan), T’boli

lakeSebu.description: A highland lake at ~1,000 m and the cultural home of the T’boli dreamweavers — known for tilapia, lotus blooms, and the T’boli Museum.
lakeSebu.name: Lake Sebu

sevenFalls.description: Lake Sebu’s crown jewel: seven cascading waterfalls and one of the highest zipline rides in the country, about a 1–1.5 hr drive from Koronadal.
sevenFalls.name: Seven Falls & Zipline, Lake Sebu

tourism.dayTripsIntro: Koronadal is a convenient base for exploring South Cotabato. These famous spots are outside the city but are commonly visited on day trips from Marbel.
tourism.festivalBody: The Hinugyaw Festival is Koronadal's signature celebration, held every January as part of Araw ng Koronadal, the city's founding anniversary. "Hinugyaw" means joyous celebration — the streets come alive with colorful street dancing, parades, and showcases of the city's settler and Indigenous cultures.
tourism.festivalNote: Dates may shift year to year. Confirm the official schedule with the City Government before planning your visit.
tourism.hotlinesCardBody: Keep important numbers handy in case you need help during your trip.
tourism.intro: Known as the "Hub of the South" and the capital of South Cotabato, Koronadal City (Marbel) blends a lively urban center with highland views, cultural heritage, and easy access to some of Mindanao's most celebrated natural wonders. Here is a guide to what to see in the city and nearby.

carpenterHill.description: A highland agri-tourism spot in Brgy. Carpenter Hill with pineapple plantations and farm views, a short drive from the city centre.
carpenterHill.name: The Farm at Carpenter Hill

cityPlaza.description: The central civic plaza in the heart of the city — a gathering place for events, evening strolls, and city celebrations.
cityPlaza.name: Koronadal City Plaza (Rizal Park)

communityMuseum.description: On the Capitol grounds, the provincial museum houses artifacts and crafts of the Blaan, T’boli, and settler communities that shaped the region.
communityMuseum.name: South Cotabato Community Museum

dioceseMarbel.description: Koronadal is the seat of the Catholic Diocese of Marbel. Parish churches across the city anchor major religious feasts and patronal fiestas.
dioceseMarbel.name: Diocese of Marbel & city churches

kalonBarak.description: An elevated viewpoint at roughly 2,375 ft offering panoramic views toward Mt. Matutum, Mt. Apo, and a sea of clouds on cool mornings — a favourite for sunrise and sunset.
kalonBarak.name: Kalon Barak Skyline Ridge

kccMall.description: The city’s main shopping mall — local restaurants, regional produce like mangoes and tilapia, and SOCCSKSARGEN souvenirs.
kccMall.name: KCC Mall of Marbel

localMarkets.description: As the "Hub of the South," Koronadal is a trade center for South Cotabato — explore its public markets, agricultural produce, and Mindanao flavors.
localMarkets.name: Local markets & cuisine

paraisoVerde.description: Billed as the largest recreational water park in the SOCCSKSARGEN region, with pools, slides, and accommodations for a family day out.
paraisoVerde.name: Paraiso Verde Resort & Water Park

provincialCapitol.description: The seat of the provincial government. Koronadal is the capital of South Cotabato and the regional center of SOCCSKSARGEN.
provincialCapitol.name: South Cotabato Provincial Capitol

tourism.seoDescription: Discover what to see and do in the City of Koronadal (Marbel), the Hub of the South — the Hinugyaw Festival, the City Plaza, Kalon Barak Skyline Ridge, and day trips to Lake Sebu and Seven Falls.
tourism.seoKeywords: Koronadal tourism, Marbel attractions, Hinugyaw Festival, Kalon Barak Skyline Ridge, Lake Sebu, Seven Falls, Lake Holon, South Cotabato, SOCCSKSARGEN, things to do in Koronadal
tourism.seoTitle: Tourism in Koronadal City — Festivals, Attractions & Day Trips
tourism.title: Tourism in Koronadal City

transparency.disclaimer: Note: BetterKoronadal.org is an independent community project and does not host official financial documents. The links below point to the official government portals that publish them.
transparency.heading: Transparency & Open Data
transparency.intro: Local government units in the Philippines are required to publish their budgets, finances, and projects under the Full Disclosure Policy (FDP). This page gathers the official channels where you can review how the City of Koronadal manages public funds.

budgetReports.description: Annual budgets, statements of receipts and expenditures, and other financial documents are posted to the LGU's FDP page and official website.
budgetReports.name: Budget & Financial Reports

cityWebsite.description: The City Government of Koronadal posts official issuances, news, and transparency documents on its own website.
cityWebsite.name: Official City Website

coa.description: Independent annual audit reports on the finances of LGUs and other government agencies, published by the Commission on Audit.
coa.name: Commission on Audit (COA) Reports

fdpPortal.description: The DILG portal where LGUs post budgets, finances, bids, and the status of programs and projects, as required by DILG Memorandum Circular 2011-134.
fdpPortal.name: Full Disclosure Policy (FDP) Portal

foi.description: Request government records through the national eFOI platform. FOI covers documents held by government agencies, subject to the standard exceptions.
foi.name: Freedom of Information (FOI)

philgeps.description: The Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System lists invitations to bid, awards, and notices to proceed under RA 9184 (Government Procurement Reform Act).
philgeps.name: PhilGEPS — Bids & Procurement

transparency.seoDescription: Where to find Koronadal's budgets, financial reports, bids, and procurement notices — the Full Disclosure Policy (FDP) Portal, PhilGEPS, COA reports, and Freedom of Information.
transparency.seoKeywords: Koronadal transparency, Full Disclosure Policy, FDP, PhilGEPS, COA, Freedom of Information, FOI, budget, procurement, open data, DILG
transparency.seoTitle: Transparency & Open Data — City of Koronadal
transparency.whatFdpCoversHeading: What the Full Disclosure Policy covers
transparency.whatFdpCoversP1: Under DILG Memorandum Circular 2011-134, every province, city, and municipality must post key financial documents in three places: a conspicuous bulletin board at the LGU hall, its official website, and the DILG's Full Disclosure Policy Portal.
transparency.whatFdpCoversP2: Disclosed documents typically include the annual budget, the statement of receipts and expenditures, the annual procurement plan, bid results, and the status of programs and projects funded by the LGU. Together these let residents see how public money is planned and spent.

provincialOfficials.title: South Cotabato Provincial Officials
provincialOfficials.intro: Profiles of the elected provincial officials of South Cotabato serving the {{term}} term — the Governor, Vice Governor, congressional representatives, and members of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (Provincial Board). Each profile lists their background, achievements, and notable notes, with sources. Koronadal City lies in the 2nd District.
provincialOfficials.empty: Profiles are being compiled and will appear here once verified against reputable sources.
provincialOfficials.legalDisclaimer: Legal matters are summarized from public reporting and official records. A filed case or complaint is an allegation only; all persons are presumed innocent unless and until a final ruling. Where a case has been dismissed or resulted in acquittal, that outcome is noted. Please consult the linked sources for the full record.
provincialOfficials.notFoundBody: We could not find a profile for that official. They may not be listed yet, or the link may be incorrect.
provincialOfficials.seoTitle: South Cotabato Provincial Officials — Governor, Vice Governor, Board & Representatives
provincialOfficials.seoDescription: Profiles of South Cotabato’s elected provincial officials: Governor, Vice Governor, congressional representatives, and Sangguniang Panlalawigan (Provincial Board) members, with achievements, notable notes, and sources.
provincialOfficials.seoKeywords: South Cotabato officials, South Cotabato governor, vice governor, Sangguniang Panlalawigan, provincial board, congressional representatives, Koronadal, SOCCSKSARGEN
provincialOfficials.sourceNote: Officials verified against election results and reputable news sources. Re-verify after any election or succession.
